subject: Mathematics
title: Brahmagupta and the Power of Zero
description: A 60-second lesson on Brahmagupta and the Power of Zero.

hook.text: In 628 AD, an Indian astronomer did what no one had dared—he wrote rules for calculating with nothing.

buildup.text: Brahmagupta defined zero as a number and wrote rules for adding, subtracting, and multiplying it.

discovery.text: He also described negative numbers as debts and positive numbers as fortunes.

twist.text: He got one rule wrong: he said zero divided by zero equals zero. That debate continues today.

climax.text: Without his work, place-value arithmetic and modern computing would not exist.

punchline.text: He gave nothing a value and changed everything.

quiz.question: What was Brahmagupta's key contribution?
options: Defining rules for computing with zero
